content:
# Seismology

**Summary**: Broad research theme addressed in the thesis collection.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]] contributed to the theme of Seismology

- [[2014-kola|Kola, 2014]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2015-latif|Latif, 2015]] analyzed the seismicity and ground motion of Java Island.

- [[2016-edigbue|Edigbue, 2016]] utilized seismic interferometry to improve SNR and enhance data quality.

- [[2018-abdulrahman|Abdulrahman, 2018]] applied seismology principles to evaluate velocity determination methods.

- [[2018-putra|Putra, 2018]] analyzed seismic array responses to impulsive signals.

- [[2020-alzahrani|Alzahrani, 2020]] focused on seismology to improve velocity analysis in the t2-x2 domain.

- [[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021]] conducted research in the field of seismology to improve automated picking.

- [[2021-shaheen|Shaheen, 2021]] focused on the theme of seismology to improve detection algorithms.

- [[2022-awais|Awais, 2022]] focused on seismic data processing techniques for first arrival detection.

- [[2023-bargees|Bargees, 2023]] investigated seismology techniques for automated first arrival picking in zero-phase data.

- [[2023-almarzooq|Almarzooq, 2023]] applied seismic processing techniques to improve VSP data inversion.

## Key Subtopics



- [[2014-kola|Kola, 2014]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2015-latif|Latif, 2015]] analyzed the seismicity and ground motion of Java Island.

- [[2016-edigbue|Edigbue, 2016]] utilized seismic interferometry to improve SNR and enhance data quality.

- [[2018-abdulrahman|Abdulrahman, 2018]] applied seismology principles to evaluate velocity determination methods.

- [[2018-putra|Putra, 2018]] analyzed seismic array responses to impulsive signals.

- [[2020-alzahrani|Alzahrani, 2020]] focused on seismology to improve velocity analysis in the t2-x2 domain.

- [[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021]] conducted research in the field of seismology to improve automated picking.

- [[2021-shaheen|Shaheen, 2021]] focused on the theme of seismology to improve detection algorithms.

- [[2022-awais|Awais, 2022]] focused on seismic data processing techniques for first arrival detection.

- [[2023-bargees|Bargees, 2023]] investigated seismology techniques for automated first arrival picking in zero-phase data.

- [[2023-almarzooq|Almarzooq, 2023]] applied seismic processing techniques to improve VSP data inversion.

## Methods and Approaches Used



## Key Findings Across Sources

- contributed to the theme of Seismology ([[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2014-kola|Kola, 2014]])

- Java Island is one of the most seismically active zones due to its complex tectonics. ([[2015-latif|Latif, 2015, p. 26]])

- Supervirtual methods can improve SNR by a factor of N compared to standard interferometry. ([[2016-edigbue|Edigbue, 2016, p. 23]])

- Analysis showed that standard processing can yield accurate results even when short-offset assumptions are relaxed in specific settings. ([[2018-abdulrahman|Abdulrahman, 2018, p. 9]])

- The research focused on attenuating surface waves like ground roll using receiver arrays. ([[2018-putra|Putra, 2018, p. 26]])

- The squaring technique provides another method for picking stacking velocities and is comparable and compatible with conventional methods. ([[2020-alzahrani|Alzahrani, 2020, p. 17]])

- First arrival traveltime is essential for both reflection and refraction data processing. ([[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021, p. 14]])

- Automatic detection of low-magnitude earthquakes is increasingly important for understanding earthquake mechanisms. ([[2021-shaheen|Shaheen, 2021, p. 96]])

- Proposed hybrid DBSCAN-SVI method achieved 98.4% accuracy in synthetic and high success rates in field data. ([[2022-awais|Awais, 2022, p. 17]])

- Different wavelets are clearly differentiated from their phase spectra in the frequency domain. ([[2023-bargees|Bargees, 2023, p. 20]])

- TCN-based models were found more effective for velocity inversion than CNNs due to their dilated convolution approach. ([[2023-almarzooq|Almarzooq, 2023, p. 73]])
